sections.paragraph:
f. יִמְלֹךְ.-

(1) to reign, to be king. (Eth. መለከ፡ id.; Arab. مَلَكَ to possess, to reign; Syr. and Ch. to consult, compare Lat. consulere for judicare, statuere (whence consul), and the Old Germ. rathen for to rule, to govern; compare Niphal.) 1Ki 6:1 2Ki 24:12 25:27 followed by עַל 1Sa 8:7 12:14 Psa 47:9, and בְּ Jos 13:12, 21 Jos 13:21 (to rule over, herrfchen über), although בְּ is also not unfrequently בְּ of place, Jud. 4:2 , herrfchen zu, to reign in.

(2) to become king, 2Sa 15:10 16:8 1Ki 14:21.

Hiphil, to make king, followed by an acc. 1Sa 15:35 1Ki 1:43 dat. 1Ch 29:22 (as if to give the kingdom to any one).

Hophal הָכְזלַךְ to be made king, Dan 9:1.

Niphal, to consult, Neh 5:7 see the Syriac usage pointed out above.

The derivatives immediately follow, except מְלוּכָה, מַמְלָכָה, מַמְלָכוּת.
